questions.q: Raigad Fort is most famous as the capital of which Indian kingdom?
questions.explanation: Raigad served as the capital of the Maratha kingdom founded by Chhatrapati Shivaji.

questions.q: Which ruler was crowned at Raigad Fort in 1674?
questions.explanation: Shivaji was crowned Chhatrapati at Raigad in 1674, formally establishing the Maratha state.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Raigad Fort located?
questions.explanation: Raigad Fort stands in the Raigad district of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Raigad Fort is a fort of what type?
options: A sea fort on an island
A hill fort in the mountains
questions.explanation: Raigad is a hill fort built atop a tall, isolated hill in the Sahyadri range.

questions.q: In which mountain range is Raigad Fort situated?
questions.explanation: Raigad is located in the Sahyadri hills, part of the Western Ghats.

questions.q: What royal title did Shivaji formally take at his Raigad coronation?
questions.explanation: At Raigad in 1674 Shivaji assumed the title Chhatrapati, meaning paramount sovereign.

questions.q: In which year was Shivaji crowned at Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Shivaji's coronation took place at Raigad in 1674.

questions.q: Raigad Fort can today be reached most easily by which modern facility?
questions.explanation: A ropeway carries visitors up to the fort, sparing the long climb of steps.

questions.q: Who is buried in the samadhi (tomb memorial) located at Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Shivaji died at Raigad in 1680 and his samadhi stands within the fort.

questions.q: Raigad Fort is associated above all with which famous Maratha leader?
questions.explanation: Raigad is forever linked to Shivaji, who made it his capital and was crowned there.

questions.q: What was Raigad Fort called before Shivaji renamed it?
questions.explanation: The hill fort was earlier known as Rairi and was renamed Raigad by Shivaji.

questions.q: The main gateway of Raigad Fort is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Maha Darwaja is the grand main gate guarding the fort's approach.

questions.q: Who is traditionally credited as the chief engineer/architect who developed Raigad as Shivaji's capital?
questions.explanation: Hiroji Indulkar is credited with constructing the major buildings of Raigad for Shivaji.

questions.q: Roughly how high is the hill on which Raigad Fort sits above sea level?
questions.explanation: The fort sits atop a hill roughly 820 metres (about 2,700 feet) above sea level.

questions.q: The marketplace street built on the fort, where shops once lined a long row, is known as what?
questions.explanation: Raigad had a planned market street, the Bazaar Peth, with rows of shops on either side.

questions.q: The sheer cliff at Raigad from which condemned prisoners were reputedly hurled to their death is called what?
questions.explanation: Takmak Tok is a steep cliff point on Raigad from which prisoners were said to have been thrown.

questions.q: The Hirakani Bastion at Raigad is named after a woman who reputedly did what?
options: Climbed down the steep cliff at night to reach her child
questions.explanation: Legend says a milkmaid named Hirakani descended the sheer cliff to reach her baby, inspiring the bastion's name.

questions.q: Which English-era envoy famously attended Shivaji's coronation at Raigad and left an account of it?
questions.explanation: Henry Oxinden of the English East India Company was present at Shivaji's 1674 coronation and recorded it.

questions.q: Which large artificial water tank is found on top of Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: The Ganga Sagar lake is a major water reservoir on the fort, used for storing rainwater.

questions.q: After Shivaji's death, which year did the Mughals under Aurangzeb finally capture Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Mughal forces under Zulfiqar Khan besieged and captured Raigad in November 1689, months after Shivaji's son Sambhaji was executed earlier that year.

questions.q: The royal palace quarters of the queens at Raigad are referred to as what?
questions.explanation: The Rani Vasa, or queens' quarters, housed the royal women within the palace complex.

questions.q: The fort's royal court hall, where Shivaji held audience and his throne stood, is known as what?
questions.explanation: The Raigad darbar (royal court) faced the Nagarkhana gate, with the throne placed for the Chhatrapati's audience.

questions.q: On approximately what date did Shivaji's main coronation ceremony take place at Raigad in 1674?
questions.explanation: Shivaji's principal coronation is traditionally dated to 6 June 1674 at Raigad.

questions.q: Which learned Brahmin scholar was invited to officiate Shivaji's Vedic coronation at Raigad?
questions.explanation: The Varanasi scholar Gaga Bhatt presided over the Vedic rites of Shivaji's coronation.

questions.q: Shivaji died at Raigad Fort in which year?
questions.explanation: Shivaji died at Raigad in 1680, a few years after his coronation.

questions.q: The faithful dog said to be commemorated by a small memorial near Shivaji's samadhi at Raigad is named what?
questions.explanation: A memorial to the dog Waghya, by tradition Shivaji's loyal pet, stands near his samadhi.

questions.q: Which British officer is associated with documenting and clearing Raigad's ruins in the 19th century, after British forces took it in 1818?
questions.explanation: James Douglas wrote detailed accounts of Raigad's ruins after the British captured the fort in 1818.

questions.q: Approximately how many steps must a visitor climb to reach Raigad Fort if ascending on foot rather than by ropeway?
questions.explanation: The traditional climb to Raigad involves roughly 1,400 to 1,500 steps up the hill.

questions.q: Which temple dedicated to a form of the goddess/Shiva stands within Raigad Fort and was revered by Shivaji?
questions.explanation: The Jagdishwar Temple, dedicated to Shiva, was built within Raigad and was venerated by Shivaji.

questions.q: A second, smaller coronation ceremony for Shivaji was held at Raigad in late 1674 according to which tradition?
options: A Tantric (Shakta) rite officiated by Nishchalpuri Gosavi
questions.explanation: A second coronation following Tantric rites was conducted by Nishchalpuri Gosavi later in 1674.
